audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua Gibeonite treaty and forced-labor narrative, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for coalition response, Gibeonite deception, moldy provisions, failure to inquire of YHWH, oath, public protest, survival, curse, and woodcutter/water-drawer status.

verses.restored: It was, when all kings who were across Jordan in hill country, in Shephelah, and along all coast of Great Sea toward Lebanon—Hittite, Amorite, Canaanite, Perizzite, Hivite, and Jebusite—heard,
that they gathered together as one to fight with Joshua and Israel with one mouth.
But inhabitants of Gibeon heard what Joshua did to Jericho and Ai,
and they too acted with cunning. They went and prepared provisions and took worn sacks for their donkeys, worn and torn wineskins bound up,
worn and patched sandals on their feet, worn garments upon them, and all bread of their provisions dry and crumbly.
They went to Joshua at camp at Gilgal and said to him and men of Israel, From a far land we have come; now cut covenant with us.
Men of Israel said to Hivite, Perhaps you dwell in our midst; how then can we cut covenant with you?
They said to Joshua, Your servants are we. Joshua said to them, Who are you, and from where do you come?
They said to him, From a very far land your servants came because of name of YHWH your Divine, for we heard his report and all he did in Egypt,
and all he did to two kings of Amorite beyond Jordan, to Sihon king of Heshbon and Og king of Bashan who was at Ashtaroth.
Our elders and all inhabitants of our land spoke to us, saying, Take provisions in your hand for way and go to meet them and say to them, Your servants are we; now cut covenant with us.
This our bread was hot when we took it for provisions from our houses on day we went out to come to you; and now, look, it is dry and crumbly.
These wineskins were new when we filled them, and look, they are torn; and these garments and sandals became worn from very long way.
The men took from their provisions, but mouth of YHWH they did not ask.
Joshua made peace with them and cut covenant with them to let them live, and leaders of congregation swore to them.
It was at end of three days after they cut covenant with them that they heard they were near to them and dwelt in their midst.
Children of Israel set out and came to their cities on third day: Gibeon, Chephirah, Beeroth, and Kiriath-jearim.
Children of Israel did not strike them because leaders of congregation had sworn to them by YHWH Divine of Israel; and all congregation murmured against leaders.
All leaders said to all congregation, We swore to them by YHWH Divine of Israel; now we may not touch them.
This we will do to them and let them live, so wrath will not be against us because of oath we swore to them.
Leaders said to them, Let them live. So they became cutters of wood and drawers of water for all congregation, as leaders spoke concerning them.
Joshua called them and spoke to them, saying, Why did you deceive us, saying, We are very far from you, when you dwell in our midst?
Now cursed are you; a servant shall not be cut off from you, cutters of wood and drawers of water for house of my Divine.

They answered Joshua and said, Because it was surely told to your servants that YHWH your Divine commanded Moses his servant to give you all land and destroy all inhabitants of land from before you; so we feared greatly for our souls/lives because of you and did this thing.
Now look, we are in your hand; as good and right in your eyes to do to us, do.
He did so to them and rescued them from hand of children of Israel, and they did not kill them.
Joshua made them on that day cutters of wood and drawers of water for congregation and for altar of YHWH, to this day, at place he would choose.

translation_notes: The Gibeonites act under credible fear of extermination after hearing Jericho and Ai. Their deception is a survival strategy, not a trivial moral failure.
Verse 14 identifies Israel's procedural failure: the leaders inspect provisions but do not inquire of YHWH.
The oath is honored despite deception, preserving the principle that powerful parties remain bound by sworn protection.
The congregation protests the treaty, while leaders resist killing the protected population.
Woodcutter and water-drawer status preserves life but imposes hereditary forced labor and social subordination. Survival does not make the arrangement just.
The Gibeonites explicitly say they acted because they expected Israel to destroy all inhabitants, keeping conquest terror visible.
The text calls the arrangement a curse; modern reception must not romanticize coerced service as grateful inclusion or benevolent assimilation.
safety_note: Do not use Joshua 9 to justify slavery, caste, hereditary servitude, forced labor, exploitation of refugees, or punishment for deceptive survival under threat. Center the binding force of protection agreements, the duty to investigate before acting, preservation of life, and liberation beyond merely surviving as a subordinated labor class.
